title: lithocardium lanceolatum
intro: Lithocardium lanceolatum, also known as Digitalis purpurea, is a plant species commonly referred to as foxglove, known for its tall spikes of purple flowers. The plant contains compounds such as digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating heart conditions like atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Lithocardium lanceolatum have been found to help regulate heart rhythm, improve heart function, and reduce symptoms of heart failure, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. Lithocardium lanceolatum can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract, and is often used in herbal supplements and medications under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
